channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Albanians protest Kushner plan for Sazan Island luxury resort
item.description: Hundreds have rallied in Albania against plans by a Jared Kushner-linked investment firm to develop Sazan Island.

item.title: ‘Encroached territory’: Why India-Nepal border dispute is heating up again
item.description: Balendra Shah's recent comments about Nepal encroaching on Indian territory have revived a 200-year-old border dispute.

item.title: ‘Trapped’: Gaza patients flown to Iraq stuck in administrative limbo
item.description: Palestinian patients stripped of their documents are confined inside a Baghdad medical centre.

item.title: Belgian NGO urges India to arrest Israeli reservist over alleged war crimes
item.description: The Hind Rajab Foundation is using videos made by Eitan Gilboa and posted to social media as evidence.

item.title: Israeli attacks on Lebanon continue after Trump saying they’d stop
item.description: Israeli air strikes on southern Lebanon have continued, a day after US President said the ‘shooting’ would stop

item.title: World Cup delay for Switzerland’s Embolo while US reviews travel document
item.description: Embolo is Switzerland's first-choice forward, with 24 goals in 86 internationals, but could not join the squad.

item.title: UK Athletics fined over death of UAE Paralympian Abdullah Hayayei
item.description: Abdullah Hayayei was killed in London in 2017 while preparing for the World ​Para Athletics Championships.

item.title: Southampton backs ‘spygate’ Eckert despite world’s most lucrative game miss
item.description: Southampton back Tonda Eckert despite missing out on a playoff final for a Premier League place due to spying scandal.

item.title: Mamdani signs ‘executive order’ letting kids stay up late for NBA Finals
item.description: Mamdani signs ‘executive order’ letting kids stay up late for NBA Finals.

item.title: What are the FIFA World Cup 2026 squads for all 48 teams?
item.description: From icons bidding adieu, to upcoming stars looking to make their mark on the biggest, Al Jazeera lists all the players.

item.title: Underground drug smuggling tunnel discovered from Tijuana to San Diego
item.description: A tunnel was discovered running underneath the US-Mexico border, allegedly used to smuggle drugs and weapons.

item.title: Every world leader who has visited China in 2026 in one chart
item.description: China has hosted 26 leaders from 23 countries this year, underscoring its growing diplomatic and economic influence.

item.title: Israel kills 8 in attacks on Lebanon after Trump announces de-escalation
item.description: The deadly attacks raise concerns about the reported agreement on the cessation of hostilities.

item.title: Trump says Israel, Hezbollah to stop fighting: What we know
item.description: Netanyahu agrees to halt operations near Beirut; Hezbollah to stop attacks on Israel under new truce arrangement.

item.title: How social media is turning African life into content at a cost
item.description: Across Africa, social media redefines politics, culture, identity, and connection, blurring community and performance.

item.title: Refugee women in CAR face childbirth risks amid US funding cuts
item.description: Limited staffing and supplies force pregnant women in CAR to give birth at home, increasing risks of preventable deaths.

item.title: How the war has made Iran’s water crisis worse
item.description: Attacks on desalination plants and other water infrastructure by US and Israel have further added to the water woes.

item.title: US gunman kills six family members and takes his own life
item.description: US gunman kills six family members and takes his own life

item.title: World Cup: No ICE deployment; extra security for Iran games in Los Angeles
item.description: Federal agents will be present during the World Cup, but immigration enforcement will not occur, county sheriff says.

item.title: Massive fire engulfs Jakarta market leaving hundreds homeless
item.description: Hundreds of people have been made homeless after a huge fire tore through a market in Jakarta.

item.title: Australia, don’t conflate anti-Semitism with criticism of Israel
item.description: The anti-Semitism commission should protect Jewish Australians from hatred, not shield Israel from accountability.

item.title: How does Iran’s leadership view the emerging deal with the US?
item.description: Iranian authorities reject capitulation, but there are subtle differences between various factions.

item.title: Somalia needs a political settlement before it is too late
item.description: Somalia is entering a dangerous moment without a path to elections or a political transition.

item.title: High Court orders Kenya government to release details of Ebola facility
item.description: Hundreds of people took to the streets in the central Kenyan town of Nanyuki to protest against the planned site.

item.title: ‘Like mice in a cage’: Inside Europe’s prison overcrowding crisis
item.description: In some of Europe’s richest countries, including Belgium, overcrowding is driving a severe deterioration of conditions.
